help.name: Display name for this agent.
help.title: Job title shown in the org chart.
help.role: Organizational role. Determines position and capabilities.
help.reportsTo: The agent this one reports to in the org hierarchy.
help.capabilities: Describes what this agent can do. Shown in the org chart and used for task routing.
help.adapter: How this agent runs: local CLI (Claude/Codex/OpenCode), OpenClaw Gateway, spawned process, or generic HTTP webhook.
help.workingDirectory: Deprecated legacy working directory fallback for local adapters. Existing agents may still carry this value, but new configurations should use project workspaces instead.
help.promptTemplate: Sent on every heartbeat. Keep this small and dynamic. Use it for current-task framing, not large static instructions. Supports {{ agent.id }}, {{ agent.name }}, {{ agent.role }} and other template variables.
help.modelOverride: Override the default model used by the adapter.
help.thinkingEffort: Control model reasoning depth. Supported values vary by adapter/model.
help.chrome: Enable Claude's Chrome integration by passing --chrome.
help.dangerouslyAutoApprove: Run unattended by auto-approving adapter permission prompts when supported.
help.disableSandbox: Run Codex without sandbox restrictions. Required for filesystem/network access.
help.webSearch: Enable Codex web search capability during runs.
help.workspaceStrategy: How Paperclip should realize an execution workspace for this agent. Keep project_primary for normal cwd execution, or use git_worktree for issue-scoped isolated checkouts.
help.worktreeBaseRef: Base git ref used when creating a worktree branch. Leave blank to use the resolved workspace ref or HEAD.
help.worktreeBranchTemplate: Template for naming derived branches. Supports {{issue.identifier}}, {{issue.title}}, {{agent.name}}, {{project.id}}, {{workspace.repoRef}}, and {{slug}}.
help.worktreeRootDir: Directory where derived worktrees should be created. Absolute, ~-prefixed, and repo-relative paths are supported.
help.workspaceServices: Optional workspace runtime service definitions. Use this for shared app servers, workers, or other long-lived companion processes attached to the workspace.
help.maxTurns: Maximum number of agentic turns (tool calls) per heartbeat run.
help.command: The command to execute (e.g. node, python).
help.cliPath: Override the path to the CLI command you want the adapter to call (e.g. /usr/local/bin/claude, codex, opencode).
help.extraArgs: Extra CLI arguments for local adapters, comma-separated.
help.envVars: Environment variables injected into the adapter process. Use plain values or secret references.
help.bootstrapPrompt: Only sent when Paperclip starts a fresh session. Use this for stable setup guidance that should not be repeated on every heartbeat.
help.requestPayload: Optional JSON merged into remote adapter request payloads before Paperclip adds its standard wake and workspace fields.
help.webhookUrl: The URL that receives POST requests when the agent is invoked.
help.heartbeatInterval: Run this agent automatically on a timer. Useful for periodic tasks like checking for new work.
help.timeoutSec: Maximum seconds a run can take before being terminated. 0 means no timeout.
help.interruptGraceSec: Seconds to wait after sending interrupt before force-killing the process.
help.wakeOnDemand: Allow this agent to be woken by assignments, API calls, UI actions, or automated systems.
help.cooldownSec: Minimum seconds between consecutive heartbeat runs.
help.maxConcurrentRuns: Maximum number of heartbeat runs that can execute simultaneously for this agent.
help.budgetMonthly: Monthly spending limit in cents. 0 means no limit.

newAgent.title: Create a new agent
newAgent.placeholder: (type in what kind of agent you want here)
newAgent.ceoRecommendation: We recommend letting your CEO handle agent setup — they know the org structure and can configure reporting, permissions, and adapters.
newAgent.askCeo: Ask the CEO to create a new agent
newAgent.chooseAdapter: Choose your adapter type for advanced setup.

config.capabilitiesPlaceholder: Describe what this agent can do...
config.promptTemplatePlaceholder: You are agent {{ agent.name }}. Your role is {{ agent.role }}...
config.promptTemplateWarning: Prompt template is replayed on every heartbeat. Keep it compact and dynamic to avoid recurring token cost and cache churn.
config.promptTemplateHint: Prompt template is replayed on every heartbeat. Prefer small task framing and variables like {{ context.* }} or {{ run.* }}; avoid repeating stable instructions here.
config.codexThinkingWarning: Codex may reject `minimal` thinking when search is enabled.
config.bootstrapPromptPlaceholder: Optional initial setup prompt for the first run
config.bootstrapPromptDeprecation: Bootstrap prompt is legacy and will be removed in a future release. Consider moving this content into the agent's prompt template or instructions file instead.
config.envVarAutoInjected: PAPERCLIP_* variables are injected automatically at runtime.
config.noHermesModel: No Hermes model detected yet. Configure Hermes or enter a provider/model manually.

choosePath.title: Specify path manually
choosePath.description: Browser security blocks apps from reading full local paths via a file picker. Copy the absolute path and paste it into the input.
choosePath.macosStep2: Hold Option and right-click the folder.
choosePath.macosStep4: Paste the result into the path input.
choosePath.windowsStep1: Find the folder in File Explorer.
choosePath.windowsStep2: Hold Shift and right-click the folder.
choosePath.windowsStep4: Paste the result into the path input.
choosePath.terminalStep3: Copy the output and paste it into the path input.
